<<THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS>>

[derive] the maximum likelihood estimators [for] the parameters [mu_x], [mu_y], [sigma_x_squared], [sigma_y_squared], [and] [sigma_xy] [given] the data [as follows]:

[pre-treatment] blood pressure [X_1, X_2, ..., X_50] [and] [post-treatment] blood pressure [Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_50] [assume] [the] pre-treatment [blood] pressures [X_i] [are] normally distributed [with] mean [mu_x] [and] variance [sigma_x_squared] [and] the post-treatment [blood] pressures [Y_i] [are] normally distributed [with] mean [mu_y] [and] variance [sigma_y_squared] [let] the covariance [between] X_i [and] Y_i [be] [sigma_xy].

[the] maximum likelihood estimators [for] [mu_x] [are] [the] sample mean [of] [X_1, X_2, ..., X_50] [the] maximum likelihood estimators [for] [mu_y] [are] [the] sample mean [of] [Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_50] [the] maximum likelihood estimators [for] [sigma_x_squared] [are] [the] sample variance [of] [X_1, X_2, ..., X_50] [the] maximum likelihood estimators [for] [sigma_y_squared] [are] [the] sample variance [of] [Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_50] [the] maximum likelihood estimators [for] [sigma_xy] [are] [the] covariance [between] [X_1, X_2, ..., X_50] [and] [Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_50].

[the] hypothesis test [to] test [the] hypothesis [that] the drug [has] a significant effect [on] reducing [blood] pressure [formulate] and [conduct] a hypothesis test [using] the derived [MLEs] [state] the null and alternative [hypotheses], [the] test statistic, [and] the rejection [criteria] [at] a significance level
